creator: apost.private@gmail.com
text:
When starting xorg (xorg-server-6.9.0_6) in dual head mode, the screen messes up and the system hangs sort of (CTRL-ALT-DEL still works to reboot the system).
Single head works fine and I can use any single one of the screens, but dual head goes limbo, either Xinerama or not it doesn't matter.

My setup:
- Dell Latitude D505
- extra LCD monitor

I can work single head when I change the BusID to 0:2:1 of the secondary device "Card1" in my xorg.conf below. That effectively disables the second screen.

Here are the xorg.conf file and the Xorg log file and the output of dmesg:

#####
# xorg.conf
#####
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "MyDualHeadLayout"
	Screen         0 "Screen0" 0 0
	Screen         1 "Screen1" RightOf "Screen0"
	InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
	Option "Xinerama" "True"
EndSection

Section "Files"
	RgbPath      "/usr/X11R6/lib/X11/rgb"

	Load  "freetype"

	Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"

	Identifier   "Monitor0"
	VendorName   "LPL"
	ModelName    "0"
EndSection

	VendorName   "LPL"

EndSection

	Screen	   0

	BusID       "PCI:0:2:0"
EndSection
